title: SplitButtonControl Object
description: A split button has two active areas that the user can click; the main button portion and the drop-down arrow. Clicking the main button, executes the displayed command. Clicking the drop-down displays the drop-down with additional commands.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: additionalDefinitions
properties.description: Gets or sets the command definitions used to define the buttons associated with the split button.

properties.name: defaultCommandDefinition
properties.description: Gets the command definition that is used as the default command on the main portion of the split button.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Gets the unique ID of this control. The ID is unique with respect to the other controls within the same panel, toolbar, or drop-down control.

properties.name: index
properties.description: Gets the position of this control within the list of controls within the panel, toolbar, or drop-down control.

properties.name: isLastUsedShown
properties.description: Gets if this button behaves where the last executed command becomes the command on the main portion of the split button.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: isVisible
properties.description: Gets or sets if this control is currently visible.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parent
properties.description: Gets the Parent object. When associated with a toolbar (right or left QAT or the NavBar) this returns the parent Toolbar object. When associated with a panel it returns the parent ToolbarPanel object. When associated with a control (DropDownControl) it returns the parent control.

related_samples.name: Write user interface to a file API Sample
related_samples.description: Writes out the full structure of the Fusion user interface. This information is useful when editing the user-interface, as discussed in the usre manual topic User-Interface Customization with Fusion's API

additionalDefinitions.title: SplitButtonControl.additionalDefinitions Property
additionalDefinitions.description: Gets or sets the command definitions used to define the buttons associated with the split button.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = splitButtonControl_var.additionalDefinitions

# Set the value of the property.
splitButtonControl_var.additionalDefinitions = propertyValue
